education.degree: Doctor of Philosophy in Environmental Engineering and Science
education.description: Specialized in industrial biotechnology, bioproduction processes and applied microbiology. Research focused on the design of a scalable industrial process for ectoine bioproduction and the use of novel extremophiles that produce extremolyte cocktails to enhance industrially relevant in vitro bacterial growth as supplemental additives.
achievements: Water Technologies Entrepreneurship and Research Institute (WaTER) Graduate Research Fellow
President of the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department Graduate Student Asociation
Director of Science Communication in the NEWT Student and Postdoc Leadership Council
Graduate level course Teaching Assistant

education.degree: Master of Science in Environmental Engineering and Science
education.description: Specialized in environmental biotechnology, wastewater treatment and molecular biology. Thesis focused on the development of a rapid and portable analytical method for in-field detection of fusobacterium necrophorum in cattle ruminal samples.
achievements: Nanotechnology-Enabled Water Treatment Institute (NEWT) Graduate Research Fellow
Director of Professional Development of the Graduate Student Asociation
Graduate level course Teaching Assistant
NSF-Funded ERC Fellowship Recipient

education.description: Comprehensive education in environmental engineering principles, with focus on water resource management, contaminated site characterization and environmental remediation. Thesis focused on developing a proposal for a remediation plan for the Venice Lagoon, Condega, Esteli, Nicaragua.
achievements: Central American University and Condega Municipal Goverment sponsored Thesis
Laboratory supervisor of volunteers for environmental project
Graduated with Honors and academic excellence
Molecular Biology Center Research Intern

experience.title: Teaching Assistant
responsibilities: Assisted instruction and guidance for graduate students in microbial kinetics concepts, bioreactor design methods, and environmental biotechnology applications in wastewater treatment.
Provided technical mentoring and problem-solving support during coursework and office hour sessions.
experience.detailedDescription: Teaching assistant for the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department of the George R. Brown School of Engineering at Rice University for a graduate-level course of environmental biotechnology (CEVE 536) during fall semesters of 2024 and 2025, focused on environmental biotechnology wastewater treatment applications and bioreactor design.
achievements: Mentored graduate students in advanced environmental biotechnology and bioreactor engineering concepts.
Strengthened student understanding of microbial kinetics modeling and wastewater treatment process design.
Supported academic and technical project development through one-on-one mentoring sessions.

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.company: Molecular Biology Center | Central American University
responsibilities: Conducted environmental water sampling and microbiological isolation of gram-negative bacteria.
Performed 16S rRNA PCR amplification and molecular identification of environmental isolates.
Analyzed extended-spectrum β-lactamase (ESBL) genes using Sanger sequencing.
Contributed to a peer-reviewed publication in the Brazilian Journal of Biology (2024).
experience.detailedDescription: Contribution to Research Project: Extended-spectrum β-lactamases genes in Gram-negative isolates from an urban river in Nicaragua, published in the Brazilian Journal of Biology (2024).
achievements: Contributed to a pioneering regional study on ESBL-producing Gram-negative bacteria in an urban river system.
Generated actionable environmental health insights through field sampling and microbiological analysis.

experience.title: Project Manager
experience.company: Inter-Ecclesial Center for Theological and Social Studies (CIEETS)
responsibilities: Formulated and Managed a rapid disaster response project following Hurricanes ETA and IOTA in rural communities in Condega, Esteli, and Totogalpa, Madriz.
Implemented community initiatives for risk-reduction and environmental resilience to disasters.
Trained community leaders in water purification methods and hydrosanitary risk reduction.
Coordinated emergency logistics and resource distribution to affected communities.
Implemented sustainable agriculture strategies for climate adaptation in agricultural systems.
Trained producers in La Conquista, Carazo, in agricultural sustainability and climate adaptation.
Coordinated logistics and distributed supplies to improve agricultural productivity and resilience.
experience.detailedDescription: As a project manager in CIEETS I formulated and managed two projects related to climate adaptation and sustainable agriculture in Nicaragua’s Dry Corridor and the rapid disaster response to ETA & IOTA hurricanes in Northern Nicaragua.
achievements: Secured and managed funding for rapid disaster-response initiatives in rural communities after Hurricanes ETA and IOTA.
Improved water and food security for vulnerable communities through emergency aid distribution and agricultural support.
Led multi-stakeholder coordination across communities, local leaders, and partner organizations.

academicRoles.role: President of the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department Graduate Student Association
academicRoles.description: President of the Graduate Student Association (GSA) of the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department at Rice University during the 2024-2025 academic year.
achievements: Collaborated with an international and multicultural team.
Connected laboratories accross the department.
High participation to organized events and seminars.

academicRoles.role: Director of Science Communication in the NEWT Student and Postdoc Leadership Council
academicRoles.institution: Nanotechnology-Enabled Water Treatment Institute (NEWT) | Rice University | Houston, TX, USA
academicRoles.description: Director of Science Communication in the Student and Postdoc Leadership Council of the Nanotechnology-Enabled Water Treatment Institute (NEWT) during the 2024-2025 academic year and a Graduate Student fellow during the 2022-2025 academic years.
achievements: NSF-Funded NEWT research associate from 2022-2025.
Created the graduate students research Highlight section in NEWT communications.
Poster presentation in inter-university conferences.

academicRoles.institution: Water Technologies Entrepreneurship and Research Institute (WaTER) | Rice University | Houston, TX, USA
academicRoles.description: Graduate Student of the Rice Water Technologies Entrepreneurship and Research Institute (WaTER) during spring 2024 and the 2024-2025 academic year.
achievements: NSF funded WaTER research associate from 2024-2025.
First wave of research associates.
Inter-University research collaboration.

researchProjects.title: Novel extremophiles produce extremolyte cocktails that enhance industrially relevant in vitro bacterial growth as supplemental additives
researchProjects.funding: Rice Water Technologies Entrepreneurship and Research Institute | Rice University
researchProjects.description: Doctoral research focused on isolating and characterizing extremophilic bacteria capable of producing extremolytes, and evaluating their application as growth-enhancing additives for industrially relevant microorganisms.
objectives: Isolated extremophilic bacteria from soil and wastewater.
Identified bacterial isolates through 16S rRNA gene sequencing.
Performed whole-genome Nanopore sequencing to characterize novel isolates and genetic adaptations.
Quantified the extremolyte production with metabolomic analysis.
Identified novel strains of Alkalicoccobacillus and Nesterenkonia that produce extremolytes.
Demonstrated that extremolyte cocktails enhanced the growth of microorganisms used in industrial bioprocesses, including Zymomonas mobilis, Pseudomonas putida, and Bacillus spp.

researchProjects.title: Development of an industrial bioprocess for ectoine bioproduction
researchProjects.funding: Nanotechnology-Enabled Water Treatment Institute | Rice University
researchProjects.description: Doctoral research focused on developing and optimizing an industrial bioprocess for sustainable ectoine and hydroxyectoine production, including upstream cultivation, analytical quantification, and downstream purification.
objectives: Modified HPLC and LC-MS/MS analytical methods for the detection of ectoine and hydroxyectoine.
Optimized intracellular and extracellular ectoine production in extremophilic bacteria.
Demonstrated extracellular ectoine production at laboratory scale in a 10-L bioreactor.
Designed a low-energy purification strategy for ectoine and hydroxyectoine recovery.

researchProjects.title: Development of a rapid and portable analytical method for in-field detection of Fusobacterium necrophorum in cattle ruminal samples
researchProjects.funding: Nanotechnology-Enabled Water Treatment Institute | Rice University
researchProjects.description: Master’s thesis research focused on developing a rapid, portable, and field-compatible molecular detection method for Fusobacterium necrophorum in cattle ruminal samples.
objectives: Developed a field-compatible DNA extraction method for cattle rumen samples using dipstick, magnetic bead, and direct elution techniques.
Conducted PCR, qPCR, LAMP, and qLAMP assays to standardize the detection of F. necrophorum.
Adapted an open-source colorimeter for real-time spectrometric and fluorometric detection of DNA amplification.
Designed an analytical method for quantitative DNA detection in field conditions for F. necrophorum.

researchProjects.title: Extended-spectrum β-lactamases genes in Gram-negative isolates from an urban river in Nicaragua
researchProjects.funding: Molecular Biology Center | Central American University
researchProjects.description: Research project focused on environmental monitoring and molecular characterization of antimicrobial resistance genes in Gram-negative bacteria isolated from Tipitapa River in Managua, Nicaragua
objectives: Conducted environmental water sampling and microbiological isolation of gram-negative bacteria.
Performed 16S rRNA PCR amplification and molecular identification of environmental isolates.
Analyzed extended-spectrum β-lactamase (ESBL) genes using Sanger sequencing.
Contributed to a peer-reviewed publication in the Brazilian Journal of Biology (2024).

researchProjects.title: Remediation Plan for Venice Lagoon in Condega, Estelí, Nicaragua
researchProjects.funding: Central American University | Condega Municipal Goverment
researchProjects.description: Bachelor’s thesis focused on assessing water quality, watershed nutrient loading, and ecosystem health to design a remediation and restoration strategy for Venice Lagoon.
objectives: Conducted water quality monitoring and physicochemical analysis of water, soil, and sediment samples.
Performed a bathymetry using manual measurement methods during the dry season of 2021.
Applied the InVEST environmental models to evaluate the nitrogen and phosphorus delivery ratio from the basin into the lagoon and the ecosystem quality of the basin.
Developed a remediation strategy for lagoon restoration in collaboration with municipal authorities.
